# **Neurobiology**

## **Molecular and Cellular Mechanisms**

* The Action Potential is an L0 "all-or-nothing" electrical signal generated by the rapid flux of ions across the axonal membrane | Electrophysiology | 0.98  
* Voltage-gated ion channels (Sodium/Potassium) act as L1 biological transistors, enabling signal propagation | Ion Channels | 0.97  
* The Resting Membrane Potential (typically \-70mV) is maintained by the L1-driven Sodium-Potassium pump | Cell Physiology | 0.96  
* Exocytosis is the L0 process by which synaptic vesicles release neurotransmitters into the synaptic cleft | Neurotransmission | 0.95  
* Saltatory Conduction allows signals to "jump" between Nodes of Ranvier, an L1 optimization for signal speed | Signal Propagation | 0.96

## **Synaptic Plasticity and Signaling**

* Hebbian Theory states that "neurons that fire together, wire together," the biological L1 basis for associative learning | Plasticity | 0.94  
* Long-Term Potentiation (LTP) is the persistent strengthening of synapses based on recent patterns of activity | Learning | 0.93  
* NMDA receptors act as L1 "coincidence detectors," requiring both ligand binding and depolarization to open | Molecular Signaling | 0.94  
* Second Messenger systems (like cAMP) amplify L0 extracellular signals into L1 intracellular responses | Signal Transduction | 0.93  
* Dendritic Spines are the L0 structural sites of most excitatory synapses, where L2 morphological changes occur | Neuroanatomy | 0.91

## **Neurobiology Meta-Context**

* The "Neural Code" is the L3 heuristic framework for understanding how temporal spikes translate to L5 meaning | Information Theory | 0.88  
* Metabolic constraints of the brain (consuming \~20% of body energy) limit the L1 bandwidth of concurrent processing | System Metabolism | 0.95  
* Retrograde signaling (e.g., via endocannabinoids) represents an L1 feedback loop for regulating synaptic strength | Cybernetics | 0.92
